title: Store Generated SQL Code as Base64 in Database
description: SQL code generated within the app (e.g., ChatGPT-generated database structures) should be encoded as base64 before being stored in the database. This follows best practices by not storing executable SQL code in clear text, reducing injection risk and improving data handling safety.
acceptance_criteria: SQL code is base64-encoded before being stored in the database
SQL code is decoded from base64 when retrieved for display or execution
Existing stored SQL is not broken by the encoding change
middleware_changes: SQL code encoding/decoding logic added to the storage and retrieval pipeline.
data_model_changes: SQL code columns now store base64-encoded content instead of raw SQL.
implementation_notes: Applies to SQL responses generated by ChatGPT for database structure generation. Base64 encoding prevents accidental SQL injection from stored content.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed the change applies to ChatGPT-generated SQL responses and implemented base64 encoding.
